policy_statement: Ban outdoor work from 11:00 AM to 16:00 PM during June through August under a national law issued in 2005 (occupational heat protection).
verbatim_text: “the Ministry of Social Affairs and Labor of Kuwait issued a national law in 2005 to stop any outdoor work from 11:00 AM to 16:00 PM during the months of June through August.”
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (national law) with specific time restriction and period.
reasoning: The verbatim text describes an enforceable national law that prohibits outdoor work during specified hours (11:00–16:00) over a defined seasonal window (June–August). This is a concrete, auditable restriction (deliverable), includes an explicit timeline window and a quantifiable operational target (specific hours and months), and uses strong, mandatory language (“issued a national law… to stop any outdoor work”). It is spatially specific at the national level (Kuwait).
validation_reasoning:
Auditable commitment: The text specifies a direct, enforceable prohibition: "issued a national law in 2005 to stop any outdoor work" (clear deliverable, not merely a study/plan).
Quantifiable target: The restriction is numerically defined: "from 11:00 AM to 16:00 PM" and applies "during the months of June through August" (measurable compliance condition).
Timeline: A concrete recurring time window is provided: "during the months of June through August" and daily hours "from 11:00 AM to 16:00 PM" (explicit temporal bounds).
Binding mechanism: Explicitly stated as a "national law" issued by the ministry (enforceable authority).
Spatial specificity: The policy is national and tied to a country context: "of Kuwait" (where it applies).
Language strength: Mandatory phrasing "national law" and "to stop any outdoor work" indicates requirement; no weak verbs like encourage/explore appear.
